articles.title: What Is a Collation, and Why Is My Data Corrupt? – PG Phridays with Shaun Thomas
articles.summary: Postgres has relied on the OS to handle text sorting for most of its history. When glibc 2.28 shipped in 2018 with a major Unicode collation overhaul, every existing text index built under the old rules became invalid... but silently. No warnings, no errors. Just wrong query results and missed rows.Postgres 17 added a builtin locale provider that removes the external dependency entirely:initdb --locale-provider=builtin --locale=C.UTF-8This change helps sorting to become stable across OS upgrades
